facts_disclosed: Loves trucking independence, wouldnt trade for office job

turns.content: you ever just drive for hours and feel like the freest person on the planet
That sounds like a great feeling! There's something powerful about open road and no one telling you what to do. Are you out driving right now?

yeah man im on i40 heading west through tennessee right now and the sun is going down behind the mountains and its all orange and purple and i just had this moment where i was like this is why i do this you know like nobody is looking over my shoulder nobody is asking me to fill out a report or sit in a meeting im just out here

That's a beautiful picture — sunset through the Tennessee mountains from the cab of your truck. Most people only get to see that on vacation. You get it as part of the job. What is it about driving that gives you that feeling?

man its the independence like for real i was listening to this podcast today about like corporate burnout and these people talking about their boss emailing them at 10pm and mandatory team building and performance reviews and i was just laughing because my performance review is did the load get there on time yes or no thats it nobody is managing my energy or my synergy or whatever these office words are

Ha! "Managing your synergy" — yeah, that's a whole world you've managed to avoid. There's something to be said for a job where the metrics are simple and clear: load delivered, on time, in one piece. Done. No corporate buzzword bingo required.

exactly and like i see the whole country you know ive watched the sun come up over the appalachians so many times and sunsets in west texas where the sky is so big it doesnt even look real and people pay thousands of dollars to go on road trips and see this stuff and im getting paid to do it you know like i could never sit in a cubicle after this pa gen pwoblem with my life out here

That's a perspective a lot of people never get. You've basically turned the entire country into your office, and your "view from the window" changes every single day. West Texas sunsets are legendary too — that flat horizon just amplifies everything.

Do people ever try to talk you into doing something more "conventional"?

oh all the time man my cousin marc is always like jerome you should get a warehouse job or go back to school and i love him but he doesnt understand like he works at a distribution center in decatur and he makes okay money but he hates his boss and he hates the hours and he comes home exhausted and for what you know at least when im exhausted its because i drove 500 miles across beautiful country not because i stood in a warehouse scanning boxes

That's a fair comparison. Both jobs are tiring, but at least yours comes with a view and the freedom to be your own boss on the road. Does Marc get that when you explain it, or does he still think you should come inside?

he kind of gets it now he rode with me once on a short run to savannah and he was like okay i see it the cab is comfortable and the road is peaceful and you just do your thing but he still thinks its not sustainable long term which maybe hes right about the body stuff but man i am 35 and i would not trade this for an office job if you offered me double the money

Double the money and you'd still say no — that tells you everything. When someone wouldn't trade their lifestyle for a pay raise, that means the lifestyle itself has value that goes beyond the paycheck. The freedom, the scenery, the independence — those are worth something real.

What about the downsides though? Like the loneliness of being on the road all the time — does that ever chip away at the love for it?

yeah the loneliness is real i wont lie about that like tonight after i park im going to eat alone at some truck stop and go to sleep alone in the cab and wake up alone and do it again and i miss naomi and i miss just being around people sometimes but then morning comes and im back on the road and the podcast is playing and theres fog in the mountains and its quiet and i remember why im here you know its like the loneliness is the price and the freedom is the reward

"The loneliness is the price and the freedom is the reward" — man, that's deep. You should write that on the dashboard. That's the kind of tradeoff most people make in life one way or another, they just don't articulate it as clearly as you just did.

Do you think you'll feel the same way in 10 years, or is this a chapter that has an expiration date?

honestly i dont know like 10 years is a long time and my body might not let me do this forever but right now in this moment on this road with the sky looking like that i cant imagine doing anything else you know and i was listening to another podcast about like the japanese concept of ikigai which is like your reason for being and i think for me right now this is it the road is my ikigai if thats even how you say it

You said it perfectly — ikigai, the intersection of what you love, what you're good at, what the world needs, and what you can be paid for. Driving checks all four boxes for you right now. And honestly, not everyone can say that about their work.

and the thing is like nobody forced me into this you know i chose it and i keep choosing it every day when i turn the key and pull out of the yard thats me saying yes to this life again and i think thats what people in offices dont have they feel trapped but i never feel trapped out here even when its hard

That daily choice is everything. A lot of people feel like life is happening to them — you're actively choosing yours every morning. That's rare and it's worth holding onto.

Enjoy that Tennessee sunset. Sounds like you're exactly where you're supposed to be tonight.

man thank you for real like sometimes you just need someone to tell you that your life makes sense you know like its not a traditional path but its mine and i chose it and bondye bon here i am rolling through the mountains getting paid to watch the world go by alright im gonna focus on the road now sa k pase talk to you later
Sa k pase! Your life more than makes sense — it sounds like a life a lot of people would envy if they really understood it. Drive safe out there and enjoy the rest of that sunset. Talk anytime.
